title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Trump says Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, contradicting Tehran
summary: Crypto analyst Michaël van de Poppe said a peace deal, which would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, would likely send liquidity back to risk-on assets such as cryptocurrencies.

title: Switzerland Asks If 10 Million People Is Where to Draw the Line
summary: A Swiss plebiscite Sunday on whether to impose a population ceiling is offering voters a choice that threatens to become their most consequential yet of the current century.

title: Mongolia Reaffirms One China Policy at Meeting, China Says
summary: Mongolian President Khurelsukh Ukhnaa said Mongolia firmly adheres to the One China principle in a meeting with China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Ulaanbaatar on June 13, according to a statement from China’s Foreign Ministry.

title: US Government Orders Anthropic to Pull Claude Fable, Mythos AI Models
summary: Anthropic pushed back against what it described as an overreach, saying the vulnerability cited is already widespread across the industry.

title: Google Sues Chinese Crime Group for Allegedly Using Gemini AI for Mass Phishing Scams
summary: Google alleges a Chinese network weaponized its Gemini AI to create phishing sites that stole millions of credit card numbers and targeted crypto investors.

title: Venice and Morpheus tokens climb as US ban on Anthropic’s Fable 5 fuels ‘permissionless AI’ pitch
summary: Venice founder Erik Voorhees and the official Morpheus account seized on the shutdown, casting it as proof of "permissionless" AI's use case.
